notable_pick_trades: Acquired GB 2026 1st (No. 20) and 2027 1st in Micah Parsons trade. Sent 2026 2nd, 2027 1st (better of two), and Mazi Smith to NYJ for Quinnen Williams. No 2nd or 3rd round picks in 2026.
roster_context: Coming off a historically bad defensive season (30.1 PPG allowed, worst in NFL) and a 7-9-1 record despite a top-5 offense. Traded Micah Parsons to GB pre-season for two 1sts and Kenny Clark, then traded for Quinnen Williams at midseason. New DC Christian Parker installing a 3-4 base scheme. Lost DE Marshawn Kneeland to tragedy in November. Offense is set with Dak Prescott, CeeDee Lamb, franchise-tagged George Pickens, and re-signed Javonte Williams. Restructured Prescott, Lamb, and Tyler Smith contracts to create ~$66M in cap space. Jerry Jones has pledged aggressive free agency spending. Two first-round picks (12 and 20) but no picks in Rounds 2-3, creating a top-heavy draft.

needs.explanation: The most glaring need on the roster. After trading Micah Parsons, the Cowboys had the worst pass defense in the NFL. Jadeveon Clowney (team-leading 8.5 sacks), Dante Fowler Jr., Sam Williams, and Payton Turner are ALL unrestricted free agents. Only Donovan Ezeiruaku and James Houston are under contract, and Ezeiruaku just had hip labrum surgery that will sideline him until training camp. The team is transitioning to a 3-4 with stand-up OLBs, and Marist Liufau is being converted to OLB as a project. Dallas needs at least one premium edge rusher via FA and one via the draft.
needs.current_starter: Donovan Ezeiruaku (recovering from hip surgery), James Houston
needs.depth: Alarmingly thin. Only two edge defenders under contract. Ezeiruaku recovering. Liufau converting from ILB — unproven.

needs.explanation: Logan Wilson was released in February, saving $6.5M. Kenneth Murray and Jack Sanborn are UFAs and won't return. The only LBs under contract are DeMarvion Overshown (injury-prone, missed 32 of 51 career games), Shemar James (promising 5th-round rookie, 91 tackles), and Marist Liufau (disappointing, now converting to OLB). Overshown has enormous upside but has never completed a full offseason program healthy until now. The 2026 LB draft class is strong (Sonny Styles, CJ Allen). Parker's 3-4 scheme requires two quality inside linebackers.
needs.depth: Marist Liufau (moving to OLB). Very thin after top two. Need a reliable starter next to Overshown.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (but no Round 2 pick, so Round 1 or FA)

needs.explanation: Trevon Diggs was waived during the 2025 season and the secondary was torched all year. DaRon Bland is the only proven starter but has dealt with recurring foot injuries and may be best suited for the slot. Shavon Revel Jr. (2025 3rd-round pick) missed the first 9 games recovering from a torn ACL and is unproven. Caelen Carson has disappointed. Trikweze Bridges started 2 games as an emergency option. New DC Christian Parker has a strong background developing DBs (Quinyon Mitchell, Cooper DeJean in Philly), but the cupboard is thin. Dallas needs at least one starting-caliber outside CB.
needs.current_starter: DaRon Bland, Shavon Revel Jr. (projected), Caelen Carson
needs.depth: Trikweze Bridges, Daron Bland may move to slot. No proven outside CB2 on roster.

needs.explanation: Tyler Smith signed a $96M extension and is elite at LG, but if he moves to LT full-time, the Cowboys lose a dominant guard. Tyler Booker (2025 1st-round pick) is projected to start at RG. Center Brock Hoffman is adequate but not a long-term answer. If Smith plays LT, the LG spot is an open competition. The interior could use depth and a potential starter at LG depending on Smith's position.
needs.current_starter: Tyler Smith (LG, may move to LT), Tyler Booker (RG), Brock Hoffman (C)
needs.depth: Adequate if Smith stays at LG. Thin if he moves to LT.

needs.explanation: Quinnen Williams, Kenny Clark, and Osa Odighizuwa remain on roster — three solid IDL starters. Clark's $21.5M cap hit with no guarantees is a potential cap casualty. If Clark is moved, IDL escalates. For now, tier holds at 3 — depth and contingency planning only.

Left tackle Tyler Guyton (2024 1st-round pick) had an inconsistent sophomore season and dealt with injuries. Tyler Smith was moved from LG to LT late in the season and may stay there in 2026 to shore up Dak's blindside. Terence Steele ($18.1M cap hit) is a potential cut candidate at RT — his inconsistent play and high cost make him expendable. If Smith moves to LT permanently, the Cowboys need a new starting LG. Nathan Thomas showed promise as a swing tackle. The OL situation is in flux but not as dire as the defensive needs.
needs.current_starter: Tyler Guyton (LT) or Tyler Smith (LT), Terence Steele (RT — potential cut)
needs.depth: Nathan Thomas (swing), Hakeem Adeniji (FA). If Smith moves to LT, guard becomes a need.
needs.draft_range_target: Day 3 (Round 4-5) — defensive picks will dominate Rounds 1

needs.explanation: Donovan Wilson (age 31) and Juanyeh Thomas are both headed to free agency. Malik Hooker (turning 30) has ~$7M in potential cap savings if released and could be a cap casualty. Markquese Bell has played more LB than S. The safety room is Hooker, Bell, Alijah Clark, Julius Wood, and Zion Childress — none are proven starters beyond the aging Hooker. Reed Blankenship (Parker's former safety in Philly) is a rumored FA target. This is a loaded safety draft class (Caleb Downs headlines it), but Dallas historically undervalues the position.
needs.current_starter: Malik Hooker (potential cap casualty), Markquese Bell
needs.depth: Alijah Clark (UDFA), Julius Wood, Zion Childress — all unproven
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if Caleb Downs falls, otherwise FA signing + Day 3 pick

free_agency_impact: Signed Rashan Gary (EDGE) from Green Bay Packers — $19.5M AAV
Signed Jalen Thompson (S) from Arizona Cardinals — $11.0M AAV
Signed Javonte Williams (RB) from Dallas Cowboys (re-sign) — $8.0M AAV
Signed Sam Williams (EDGE) from Dallas Cowboys (re-sign) — $3.0M AAV
Signed George Pickens (WR) from Dallas Cowboys (franchise tag) — $27.3M AAV
Signed Brandon Aubrey (K) from Dallas Cowboys (RFA tender) — $5.8M AAV
Signed T.J. Bass (IOL) from Dallas Cowboys (RFA tender) — $5.8M AAV
Signed Josh Butler (CB) from Dallas Cowboys (ERFA) — $1.1M AAV
Signed Reddy Steward (CB) from Dallas Cowboys (ERFA) — $1.0M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: New DC Christian Parker is installing a 3-4 base defense (first since 2012) that will be multiple with 4-3, 4-2-5 nickel, and 5-man front looks. This scheme change elevates the need for stand-up OLB/EDGE rushers who can drop into coverage, versatile inside linebackers, and coverage-capable safeties. Parker's Philly roots (Vic Fangio tree) emphasize two-high safety shells, pre-snap disguises, and scheme versatility. The DT room (Williams/Clark/Odighizuwa) is the one defensive strength and will anchor the front. Edge prospects who can play with their hand up in a 3-4 OLB role (not just 4-3 DEs) are ideal fits. CBs who can play man or zone from multiple alignments fit Parker's multiple coverage philosophy.
